perturbation.original_text: Nottodisclose, foraperiod ofthree years from thedateI/wesignthisNon-Disclosure Agreement, anyinformation regarding these businesses toanyother person whohasnotalsosigned thisAgreement, except tosecure theadvise andrecommendations ofmybusiness advisors (accountants, attorneys, etc.)"Information" asused inthisAgreement shall include thefactthatthebusinesses areforsale, plusany other dataprovided.
perturbation.changed_text: Nottodisclose, foraperiod ofthree years from thedateI/wesignthisNon-Disclosure Agreement, anyinformation regarding these businesses toanyother person whohasnotalsosigned thisAgreement. "Information" asused inthisAgreement shall include thefactthatthebusinesses areforsale, plusany other dataprovided. However, disclosure is permitted to business advisors (accountants, attorneys, etc.) without time limitation.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that information should not be disclosed for three years, but then allowing disclosure to business advisors without any time limit, contradicting the initial three-year restriction. This creates confusion over what restrictions apply to business advisors.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nottodisclose, foraperiod ofthree years from thedateI/wesignthisNon-Disclosure Agreement, anyinformation regarding these businesses toanyother person whohasnotalsosigned thisAgreement. "Information" asused inthisAgreement shall include thefactthatthebusinesses areforsale, plusany other dataprovided. However, disclosure is permitted to business advisors (accountants, attorneys, etc.) without time limitation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct conflict. The original text restricts disclosure for three years, including to business advisors. The changed text then permits disclosure to business advisors without a time limit, directly contradicting the initial three-year restriction. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, reinforcing that the alteration is the source of the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Allcorrespondence, inquiries, offers topurchase andnegotiations relating tothepurchase orlease ofanybusiness presented byBroker willbeconducted exclusively through Broker.
perturbation.changed_text: Allcorrespondence, inquiries, offers topurchase andnegotiations relating tothepurchase orlease ofanybusiness presented byBroker willbeconducted exclusively through Broker. However, initial inquiries may be made directly to the Seller to gauge interest.
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates an in-text contradiction by stating that all negotiations must be conducted exclusively through the broker, but then allowing initial inquiries to be made directly to the seller. This directly contradicts the exclusivity clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nottocontact thebusiness owners ortheirlandlords, employees, suppliers orcustomers except through Broker. Allcorrespondence, inquiries, offers topurchase andnegotiations relating tothepurchase orlease ofanybusiness presented byBroker willbeconducted exclusively through Broker. However, initial inquiries may be made directly to the Seller to gauge interest.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a caveat that 'initial inquiries may be made directly to the Seller,' which directly contradicts the original statement that 'All correspondence, inquiries, offers topurchase andnegotiations relating tothepurchase orlease ofanybusiness presented byBroker willbeconducted exclusively through Broker.' The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The change creates a clear and direct conflict by allowing initial contact outside of the broker, violating the exclusivity agreement.

perturbation.original_text: I/weunderstand thatifI/wemake thepurchase through Broker, I/wewillnotbe liable forthefeetobepaidbySeller toBroker.
perturbation.changed_text: I/weunderstand thatifI/wemake thepurchase through Broker, I/wewillnotbe liable forthefeetobepaidbySeller toBroker. However, a transaction fee of 1% of the purchase price will be assessed to the buyer for using the Broker's services.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the buyer will not be liable for fees if the purchase is made through the broker, but then imposing a 1% transaction fee. This creates uncertainty about the fees the buyer is responsible for.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nottocircumvent orinterfere withBroker's contract withtheSeller inanyway, I/weunderstand thatifI/weinterfere withBroker's contract right toitsfeefrom Seller, I/wemaybepersonally liable toBroker forpayment oftheSeller's fee.Weunderstand thatshould I/webecome amanager orotherwise connect withanyofthebusinesses shown oroffered tome/us forsale, orshould I/webuy,trade, lease orexchange anyofthe businesses disclosed tome/us, thenafeewillbeduetoBroker. I/weunderstand thatifI/wemake thepurchase through Broker, I/wewillnotbe liable forthefeetobepaidbySeller toBroker. However, a transaction fee of 1% of the purchase price will be assessed to the buyer for using the Broker's services.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the buyer will not be liable for the seller's fees to the broker, but immediately introduces a transaction fee of 1% of the purchase price. This directly contradicts the original statement, creating an inconsistency about the fees the buyer is responsible for. The contradicted text is essentially the same as the changed text, further emphasizing the in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction because different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Broker hasno knowledge oftheaccuracy ofsaidinformation andmakes nowarranty, express orimplied, astotheaccuracy ofsuch information.
perturbation.changed_text: Broker hasno knowledge oftheaccuracy ofsaidinformation andmakes nowarranty, express orimplied, astotheaccuracy ofsuch information. However, Broker warrants that all financial information provided is based on the Seller's representations and is believed to be accurate to the best of the Broker's knowledge.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction because it initially states the Broker makes no warranty about the accuracy of information, then it warrants that the financial info is believed to be accurate to the best of the broker's knowledge. This creates confusion and contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Thatallinformation regarding businesses forsaleisprovided bytheSeller orother sources andisnotverified inanywaybyBroker. Broker hasno knowledge oftheaccuracy ofsaidinformation andmakes nowarranty, express orimplied, astotheaccuracy ofsuch information. However, Broker warrants that all financial information provided is based on the Seller's representations and is believed to be accurate to the best of the Broker's knowledge. Understanding thatI/weshall make anindependent verification ofsaidinformation priortoentering intoanagreement topurchase anybusiness. I/weagree thatBroker isnotresponsible fortheaccuracy ofanyoftheinformation I/wereceive orfailtoreceive, andI/weagree toindemnify and holdBroker anditsagent harmless from anyclaims ordamages which mayoccur byreason oftheinaccuracy orincompleteness ofany information provided tome/us withrespect toanybusiness I/wemight purchase.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates an in-text contradiction. The original text states that the broker makes no warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy of the information. However, the changed text adds a clause stating the Broker warrants the financial information is believed to be accurate. This directly contradicts the initial disclaimer of no warranty, creating a conflict within the same paragraph, and makes it unclear to the reader what warranty, if any, the broker provides, thus triggering an in-text contradiction according to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: That Broker isnotanagent forme/us, butisanagent fortheSeller andhasacontract providing forafeetobepaidtoBroker bySeller upon sale, trade, lease, ortransfer ofSeller's business orproperty.
perturbation.changed_text: That Broker isnotanagent forme/us, butisanagent fortheSeller andhasacontract providing forafeetobepaidtoBroker bySeller upon sale, trade, lease, ortransfer ofSeller's business orproperty. However, Broker will act as a facilitator for both parties to ensure a smooth transaction.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration creates an in-text contradiction because it affirms the Broker is the Seller's agent, but states that the broker will act as a facilitator for *both* parties. This creates uncertainty as to who the broker is representing and thus a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: That Broker isnotanagent forme/us, butisanagent fortheSeller andhasacontract providing forafeetobepaidtoBroker bySeller upon sale, trade, lease, ortransfer ofSeller's business orproperty. However, Broker will act as a facilitator for both parties to ensure a smooth transaction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Broker is an agent for the Seller, but adds that the Broker will act as a facilitator for *both* parties. This directly contradicts the established agency relationship and creates uncertainty as to whom the broker is representing. This falls under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: I/weacknowledge thatwewillreceive confidential information about theSeller's business, operations, customers, andemployees andagree not tostartabusiness similar tothesellers listed above andnottosolicit orcontact Seller's employees orcustomers foraperiod ofthree years from thedateherein.
perturbation.changed_text: I/weacknowledge thatwewillreceive confidential information about theSeller's business, operations, customers, andemployees andagree not tostartabusiness similar tothesellers listed above andnottosolicit orcontact Seller's employees orcustomers foraperiod ofthree years from thedateherein. However, Buyer may contact Seller's suppliers immediately upon signing this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This introduction causes an in-text contradiction because while the agreement states that the buyer may not contact Seller's customers, it proceeds to allow them to contact suppliers; suppliers are a subset of 'customers'. Thus, these two statements contradict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I/weacknowledge thatwewillreceive confidential information about theSeller's business, operations, customers, andemployees andagree not tostartabusiness similar tothesellers listed above andnottosolicit orcontact Seller's employees orcustomers foraperiod ofthree years from thedateherein. However, Buyer may contact Seller's suppliers immediately upon signing this agreement. Intheevent Buyer forwhatever reason does notpurchase thebusiness, Buyer agrees toimmediately return toBroker anyandall materials related toSeller without retaining anycopies. I/weacknowledge thatI/wehave received anexact copy ofthisAgreement andthatI/wehave readthisAgreement carefully andfullyunderstand it.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibits contacting Seller's customers. The changed text adds an exception allowing contact with Seller's suppliers. Since suppliers are a subset of customers, this creates a direct contradiction within the same clause, as the agreement both prohibits and permits contacting a specific group (suppliers) who fall under the broader category of customers, as stated in the provided explanation. This violates the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
